2606.20164.title: MedRLM: Recursive Multimodal Health Intelligence for Long-Context Clinical Reasoning, Sensor-Guided Screening, Evidence-Grounded Decision Support, and Community-to-Tertiary Referral Optimization
2606.20164.paper_summary: Real-world clinical decision support requires reasoning over heterogeneous and longitudinal patient information rather than answering isolated medical questions. However, current medical large language models and retrieval-augmented generation systems often rely on single-step prompting or retrieval, which can be fragile when clinical evidence is distributed across long electronic health records, medical images, sensor streams, guidelines, and referral constraints. This paper proposes MedRLM, a Recursive Multimodal Health Intelligence framework for long-context clinical reasoning, sensor-guided screening, and community-to-tertiary referral support. Instead of compressing all patient information into one prompt, MedRLM treats the patient case as an external clinical environment that can be recursively inspected, decomposed, retrieved, verified, and synthesized. The framework coordinates specialized agents for clinical text, longitudinal EHR, medical imaging, physiological sensor signals, guideline retrieval, uncertainty auditing, and referral planning. It further introduces a Clinical Evidence Graph Memory to connect patient-specific observations with retrieved evidence, standardized definitions, sensor-derived biomarkers, and referral criteria. A sensor-guided recursive triggering mechanism activates deeper reasoning when abnormal physiological or behavioral patterns are detected, while uncertainty-gated refinement supports clinician review for high-risk or low-confidence cases. We also outline a real-data evaluation design using public and credentialed clinical datasets spanning EHR, radiology, ECG, ICU time series, and referral-proxy outcomes. MedRLM aims to move medical AI from static question answering toward auditable, multimodal, and workflow-aware clinical decision support.
2606.20164.paper_summary_zh: 現實世界的臨床決策支持需要對異質和長期的病人信息進行推理，而不是僅僅回答孤立的醫療問題。<lb/>然而，目前的醫療大型語言模型和檢索增強生成系統往往依賴於單步提示或檢索，這在臨床證據分布在長電子健康記錄、醫療影像、傳感器數據流、指導方針和轉診限制時可能顯得脆弱。<lb/>本文提出了MedRLM，一個用於長上下文臨床推理、傳感器引導篩查和社區到三級轉診支持的遞歸多模態健康智能框架。<lb/>MedRLM並不是將所有病人信息壓縮成一個提示，而是將病人案例視為一個可以遞歸檢查、分解、檢索、驗證和綜合的外部臨床環境。<lb/>該框架協調專門的代理來處理臨床文本、長期電子健康記錄、醫療影像、生理傳感器信號、指導方針檢索、不確定性審計和轉診規劃。<lb/>它進一步引入了一個臨床證據圖記憶，以將病人特定的觀察與檢索到的證據、標準化定義、傳感器衍生的生物標記和轉診標準相連接。<lb/>當檢測到異常的生理或行為模式時，傳感器引導的遞歸觸發機制會激活更深層次的推理，而不確定性門控的精煉則支持臨床醫生對高風險或低信心案例的審查。<lb/>我們還概述了一個使用公共和經認證的臨床數據集的真實數據評估設計，這些數據集涵蓋電子健康記錄、放射學、心電圖、重症監護時間序列和轉診代理結果。<lb/>MedRLM旨在將醫療AI從靜態問題回答推進到可審計的、多模態的和工作流程感知的臨床決策支持。
2606.20164.author: Aueaphum Aueawatthanaphisut et.al.
